zachary_wang.name: Zachary Wang
zachary_wang.summary:
A California competitor, Zachary Wang has stepped onto the championship stage, currently competing at the U15 level, reaching the finals 1 time. With 45 entries across 17 tournaments, Zachary is proving the ability to compete at the highest level.

Zachary is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a doubles best of #2, a mixed doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

With 15 different doubles partners across the career, Zachary adapts to anyone:
  • Eddie Sun — a trusted BD partner (8 events, best: #3)
  • Ethan Yip Ly — 3 events in BD, best: #3
  • Shivani Ramakrishnan — 3 events in XD, best: #4

A veteran of the junior circuit, Zachary has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Zachary has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience, Zachary brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

zack_nguyen.name: Zack Nguyen
zack_nguyen.summary:
A California competitor, Zack Nguyen has tasted victory multiple times, currently competing at the U13 level, capturing 2 titles across 11 tournaments and 28 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Zack keeps finding ways to come out on top.

What makes Zack especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

With 5 different doubles partners across the career, Zack adapts to anyone:
  • Joanna Zhang — a trusted XD partner (7 events, best: #2)
  • Keaton Ou — 4 events in BD, producing 2 titles together

Zack has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Zack has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Zack's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

zaph_j_din_bayona.name: Zaph J-Din Bayona
zaph_j_din_bayona.summary:
Representing Texas, Zaph J-Din Bayona is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-3 finish at Selection places Zaph in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Zaph is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (3 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Zaph is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, 4 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-3 (U15 BS, U15 BD) • Junior Nationals top-4 (BS U15) • 2 ORC titles.

The numbers tell a compelling story: Zaph boasts a commanding 70% win rate across 91 matches, 57 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a biggest win margin of 33 points.

Zaph has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 18 different players:
  • Arjun Aditya Hariharan — 7 events, 1 title together in BD/U13
  • Yifei Zhang — 6 events in BD, producing 2 titles together
  • Shaoyao Luo — a trusted XD partner (4 events, best: #2)

A veteran of the junior circuit, Zaph has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Zaph has competed across 4 different regions (NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 9 seeded entries across the career, Zaph consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 5 titles, Zaph is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

What makes Zechariah especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 3 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (3 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (6 entries) • ORC top-2 finisher.

With 8 different doubles partners across the career, Zechariah adapts to anyone:
  • Bryce Xie — 14 events in BD, producing 2 titles together
  • Mandy Lu — a trusted XD partner (13 events, best: #2)

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 2 titles across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Zechariah is still ascending.

Zechariah has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Zechariah isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 12 seeded entries across the career, Zechariah consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 5 titles, Zechariah is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Hailing from Washington, Zimeng Zhou* has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U17 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 3 entries in 1 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

What makes Zimeng especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

ziming_zhao.name: Ziming Zhao
ziming_zhao.summary:
Out of Texas, Ziming Zhao is writing the opening pages of a badminton story, currently competing at the U13 level, with 4 entries across 2 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

Ziming competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

zimo_zhai.name: Zimo Zhai
zimo_zhai.summary: Zimo Zhai has stepped onto the competitive stage with 1 tournament entry. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Zimo's.

ziqin_yu.name: Ziqin Yu
ziqin_yu.summary:
A California competitor, Ziqin Yu is in the early chapters of a competitive journey, currently competing at the U19 level, with 3 entries across 2 tournaments. The willingness to compete is where every great player starts.

Notably, Ziqin has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Ziqin competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

Ziqin has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

zisheng_gaogao_fan.name: Zisheng (GaoGao) Fan
zisheng_gaogao_fan.summary:
A Massachusetts competitor, Zisheng (GaoGao) Fan has reached the podium at Junior Nationals, finishing top-3 in BD U13, XD U13. At the biggest event on the calendar, making the final four is a statement: Zisheng belongs with the best in the country.

Zisheng is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (3 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

What makes Zisheng especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 2 singles titles, 3 doubles titles, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (9 entries) • Junior Nationals top-3 (BD U13, XD U13) • 3 ORC titles.

With 19 different doubles partners across the career, Zisheng adapts to anyone:
  • Michael Liu — 10 events in BD, producing 2 titles together
  • Sitar Nainish Sanghani — a trusted U13/XD partner (8 events, best: #5)
  • Jason Gu — a trusted BD partner (7 events, best: #3)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 5 podium finishes across 6 tournaments. Zisheng is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Zisheng has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Zisheng has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 22 seeded entries across the career, Zisheng consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 7 titles, Zisheng is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

zisheng_fan.name: Zisheng Fan
zisheng_fan.summary:
Hailing from Massachusetts, Zisheng Fan has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U13 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 10 entries in 4 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

Notably, Zisheng has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Zisheng is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Zisheng has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Zisheng isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Zisheng's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

ziyan_zhao.summary:

Open Regional Championships draw the top talent from across the region, and taking home the title is a genuine achievement.

Ziyan is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (5 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Ziyan is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (2 entries) • 2 ORC titles.

With 27 different doubles partners across the career, Ziyan adapts to anyone:

Ziyan has been on this journey through U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Ziyan has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 3 titles, Ziyan is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Ziyang is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #3, a doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Ziyang has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 5 different players:

Ziyang has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Ziyang brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

zmy_gao.name: Zmy Gao
zmy_gao.summary:
A Texas competitor, Zmy Gao has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-4), currently competing at the U15 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Zmy's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Zmy has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Zmy is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #3, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (3 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (9 entries) • ORC top-4 finisher.

Beyond the trophies, the numbers stand out: Zmy boasts 32 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a solid 62% win rate over 77 matches, a clutch 69% win rate in three-game matches (11/16).

Zmy has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 28 different players:
  • Liam Xiao — 6 events, 1 title together in BD/U13
  • Nihal Reddy Gajjala — a trusted BD partner (5 events)

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 1 title across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Zmy is still ascending.

Zmy has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Zmy has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Over 4 seasons and 2 titles, Zmy has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

zoey_enqi_tan.name: Zoey Enqi Tan
zoey_enqi_tan.summary:
A California competitor, Zoey Enqi Tan has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U19 GD, U15 GD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 12 career titles across 21 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Zoey is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (7 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Zoey competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 2 singles titles, 5 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U19 GD, U15 GD) — selected to represent the USA • 7 ORC titles.

With 11 different doubles partners across the career, Zoey adapts to anyone:
  • Cassidy Yeh — 16 events in GD/U15/U19, producing 6 titles together
  • Youshi Chen — a trusted U19/XD partner (4 events, best: #5)

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 2 titles across 2 tournaments — a clear sign that Zoey is still ascending.

Zoey has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Zoey has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 6 seeded entries across the career, Zoey consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 12 titles, Zoey has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.
